problem_19.natural_language: Fermat's little theorem in the form `k^p ≡ k [ZMOD p]` for prime `p`.
problem_19.lean: theorem fermat_little_zmod {p : ℕ} (hp : Nat.Prime p) (k : ℤ) : k ^ p ≡ k [ZMOD p]
problem_19.natural_language_proof:
Proof: work modulo `p`. If `k ≡ 0`, then `k^p ≡ 0 ≡ k`. Otherwise the class of `k` is a unit in
`(ZMod p)ˣ`, whose multiplicative group has order `p - 1`. Hence `k^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p)`. Multiplying
by `k` gives `k^p ≡ k (mod p)`, i.e. `k ^ p ≡ k [ZMOD p]`.
